data.title: Carrier Connect Data Solutions acquires Purecolo
data.description:
• Carrier Connect Data Solutions (CCDS:CA)  announced on Friday that it had entered into a definitive share purchase agreement on November 27, 2025, to acquire all the issued and outstanding securities of PureColo.
  • According to the agreement, the company will issue 4.60M common shares and a cash consideration of approximately $2.33M to the PureColo security holders in connection with the acquisition.
  • Cash consideration will be paid over a span of 9 months, subject to certain debt covenants of PureColo in the agreement.
  • The acquisition will expand the company geographically with the addition of 4 data centers in 3 regions.
  • Shares +8.75%.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Holiday shopping data, Amazon event, and Salesforce earnings
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - December 1

  • Volatility watch -  Cipher Mining (CIFR) and Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Haemonetics (HAE), Exact Sciences (EXAS), and Quince Therapeutics (QNCX). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Nutanix (NTNX), Northland Power (OTCPK:NPIFF), and Fiserv (FISV).
  • IPO watch - The quiet period expires on Beta Technologies (BETA), BillionToOne (BLLN), Aeromexico (AERO), and Evommune (EVMN) to free up analysts to post ratings. The IPO lock-up period expires on certain blocks of shares of Circle Internet (CRCL), Omada Health (OMDA), and zSpace (ZSPC). Some of the blocks of shares of Circle had an IPO expiration date of November 16.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include MongoDB (MDB).
  • Data watch - Key data reports due to arrive during the first few days of the week include updates on deliveries from Nio (NIO), XPeng (XPEV), Li Auto (LI), BYD Company (OTCPK:BYDDF), and ZEEKR Intelligent (ZK). Other reports of high interest to investors will be the monthly Macau gross gaming revenue release, the monthly update on Class 8 truck orders, firearm background check data from the FBI, and discount broker DARTs reports.
  • All week - Notable investor conferences during the week include the Goldman Sachs Industrials and Materials Conference, Benchmark Discovery Investor Conference, Citi Global Healthcare Conference, Barclays Eat, Sleep, Play, Shop Conference, and the UBS Global Technology and AI Conference.
  • All day - Amazon (AMZN) will hold its big annual cloud conference in Las Vegas next week. AWS re:Invent will have a heavy focus on agentic AI, infrastructure, and hands-on technical content. Notable speakers include AWS CEO Matt Garman, senior AWS engineering leaders, and Amazon CTO Dr. Werner Vogels in the closing keynote.

 

Tuesday - December 2

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Crowdstrike (CRWD), Marvell Technology (MRVL), Pure Storage (PSTG), Box (BOX), and Okta (OKTA). Options trading implies a double-digit move for American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) and GitLab (GTLB) after they report.
  • All day - Monster Beverage (MNST) CEO Hilton Schlosberg will host an investor meeting to provide an update on the company's business and operations.
  • All day - Vale (VALE) will hold its annual meeting with investors and capital market participants.
  • All day - Salesforce (CRM) will release its full Cyber Monday recap.
  • All day - Nvidia (NVDA) will participate in the 2025 NeurIPS conference. The company will have demos and sessions on AI, robotics, self-driving cars, and simulation, as well as co-host talks with partners on applied generative AI, recommendations, and other production use cases.
  • 11:00 a.m. The National Retail Federation will announce Thanksgiving weekend shopping results during a media call.

 

Wednesday - December 3

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Salesforce (CRM), Royal Bank of Canada (RY), Snowflake (SNOW), Dollar Tree (DLTR), Five Below (FIVE), Guidewire Software (GWRE), and Macy's (M). Options trading implies a double-digit move for C3.ai (AI) and Tilly's (TLYS) after they report.
  • All day - The New York Times DealBook Summit will include interviews with Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei, Coinbase (COIN) CEO Brian Armstrong, General Motors (GM) CEO Mary Barra, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) CEO David Ellison, BlackRock (BLK) CEO Larry Fink, Palantir (PLTR) CEO Alex Karp, Under Armour (UAA) CEO Kevin Plank, California Governor Gavin Newsom, and U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
  • All day - Notable investor events include Genius Sports' (GENI) Investor Day, Masimo's Investor Day (MASI), and GlobalFoundries' (GFS) Investor Meeting.
  • Genius Sports (GENI) will hold its Investor Day. The event will feature presentations from the senior leadership team, providing a comprehensive overview of the business strategy, key growth drivers, product demonstrations, and financial outlook.
  • All day - GlobalFoundries (GFS) will host a webinar for investors and analysts to provide a market, technical, and strategy update on the opportunities for the company across the rapidly evolving physical AI market.

 

Thursday - December 4

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD), Kroger (KR), Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), Ulta Beauty (ULTA), and Dollar General (DG). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Rubrik (RBRK) and Samsara (IOT) after they report.
  • All day - Rio Tinto (RIO) will hold its Capital Markets Day event.
  • All day - IMAX Corporation (IMAX) will host an investor day to detail the company’s long-term growth plan across its global content portfolio, system network, and technology platform.
  • All day - Denali Therapeutics (DNLI) will hold its Investor Day event.

 

Friday - December 5

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Victoria's Secret (VSCO).
  • All day - The go-shop period on the Golden Entertainment (GDEN) sale of operating assets to Blake Sartini and VICI Properties (VICI) ends.
  • All day - ServiceNow (NOW) shareholders will vote on the company's proposed five-for-one stock split.
  • All day - The United Nations will release its latest FAO Food Price Index and monthly grains report, which are watched by commodities traders.
  • All day - The delayed core PCE price index report for September will be released.

data.title: UPS executive warns MD-11 grounding could 'last for months,' miss holiday season -- report
data.description:
United Parcel Service’s (UPS) fleet of MD-11 aircraft, grounded in the wake of a deadly crash in Louisville, Kentucky, will not be back in service in time for the busy holiday season, leaving the company to rely on “contingency plans” to complete deliveries.

“Regarding the MD-11 fleet, Boeing’s ongoing evaluation shows that inspections and potential repairs will be more extensive than initially expected” and could take months, UPS Airlines president Bill Moore said in a memo to employees cited by the Associated Press.

On November 4, a Boeing (BA) MD-11 operated by UPS (UPS) leaving the Louisville Muhammad Ali Airport bound for Hawaii crashed in a Louisville industrial area immediately after takeoff. The crash killed 14 people, including 11 on the ground, and injured another 23 leading the Federal Aviation Administration to issue an emergency airworthiness directive to other MD-11 operators, including those planes operated by FedEx (FDX).

UPS (UPS) grounded its own fleet “out of an abundance of caution and in the interest of safety.”

The black box data shows the aircraft only reached an altitude of 30 feet before a fire in the left engine triggered the crash.

The UPS MD-11 fleet – accounting for 9% of the company’s total fleet of aircraft -- is now grounded “indefinitely” and not expected to be returned to service for several months.

data.title: B. Riley Financial receives expected Nasdaq delinquency notice
data.description:
B. Riley Financial (RILY) announced on Friday an expected Nasdaq delinquency notice over non-compliance with the listing rule on a delay in filing of financial results.

The listing rules require listed companies to timely file all required periodic financial reports with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

For the period ended September 30, 2025, the company is now required to file Form 10-Q with the SEC through no later than January 20, 2026.

B. Riley said it intends to take all appropriate actions to regain compliance with the Nasdaq listing requirements.

Shares were -0.46% post market close to $4.32.

data.title: Airbus orders software fix for 6,000 A320 jets after solar glitch, warns of passenger disruptions
data.description:
Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) said Friday a significant number of its active A320 jetliner fleet, involving ~6,000 jets, will require an immediate software fix after a recent incident involving a Jetblue airliner revealed that "intense solar radiation" could risk corrupting data critical to maintaining functioning flight controls.

The company "acknowledges these recommendations will lead to operational disruptions to passengers and customers."

Most of the jets can receive an uncomplicated update from the cockpit with minimal downtime, but ~1,000 older jets will need an actual hardware upgrade that can take much longer, according to Bloomberg.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) also said it is restricting operations in extreme cold for some aircraft equipped with RTX (RTX) Pratt & Whitney engines.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) has been forced to contend with engine issues on its newer A320neo jets, involving those built by Pratt & Whitney, that have forced hundreds of jets to be taken out of service temporarily for maintenance.

data.title: These are the top trending AI stocks according to Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings
data.description:
Technology stocks (XLK) are currently among the top gainers in the S&P 500 (SP500), with companies like Intel (INTC), Sandisk Corp. (SNDK), Western Digital (WDC), and Super Micro Computer (SMCI) leading the charge as some of the best performers.

The following list screen recently trending AI technology stocks mentioned in analysis articles within the past 60 days, ranked by Seeking Alpha’s Quant metrics.

The parameters used for selection emphasize companies that have gained attention in the artificial intelligence space while demonstrating strong quantitative metrics according to Seeking Alpha’s proprietary rating system.

For instance, Intel (INTC) shares are up more than 10% after analyst Ming-Chi Kuo suggested the chip giant could become a foundry supplier for some of Apple’s (AAPL) M-series processors, highlighting the potential benefits of “Made in USA” manufacturing policies.

The criteria prioritize companies operating primarily in the AI and Machine Learning segments, with market capitalizations ranging from smaller players like eGain Corporation (EGAIN) at $275.92M to industry giants such as Dell Technologies (DELL) at $89.31B.

Performance metrics and valuation indicators are also key components of the ranking system, providing investors with comprehensive data for decision-making.

Here are the top AI stocks by Seeking Alpha Quant ratings:

  1. Fastly, Inc. (FSLY) - Rating: Strong Buy (4.87), -1.89% YTD, $1.77B Market Cap

  10. LiveRamp Holdings, Inc. (RAMP) - Rating: Buy (3.70), -0.22% YTD, $1.84B Market Cap

data.title: Market Voices: Trump on immigration, G20; SEC probing Jefferies
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact markets, sectors, or individual stocks. 

  • President Trump said he plans to halt migration from Third World nations and denaturalize migrants "who undermine domestic tranquility" following the shooting of two National Guardsmen by an Afghan national who had been granted U.S. asylum earlier this year.

"I will permanently pause migration from all Third World Countries to allow the U.S. system to fully recover, terminate all of the millions of Biden illegal admissions, including those signed by Sleepy Joe Biden’s Autopen, and remove anyone who is not a net asset to the United States, or is incapable of loving our Country, end all Federal benefits and subsidies to noncitizens of our Country, denaturalize migrants who undermine domestic tranquility, and deport any Foreign National who is a public charge, security risk, or non-compatible with Western Civilization," Trump said in a lengthy post on Truth Social late Thursday. 

"These goals will be pursued with the aim of achieving a major reduction in illegal and disruptive populations, including those admitted through an unauthorized and illegal Autopen approval process. Only REVERSE MIGRATION can fully cure this situation," Trump added.

  • Trump also said that South Africa would not be invited to next year's G20 economic summit, which will be held in Miami.

"At my direction, South Africa will NOT be receiving an invitation to the 2026 G20, which will be hosted in the Great City of Miami, Florida next year. South Africa has demonstrated to the World they are not a country worthy of Membership anywhere, and we are going to stop all payments and subsidies to them, effective immediately," Trump said in a post on Truth Social.

The U.S. did not attend last week's G20 summit in South Africa.

  • The SEC has launched an investigation into Jefferies's (JEF) relationship with auto parts maker First Brands Group, which declared bankruptcy in September.

The Financial Times reported that the SEC is investigating, in particular, whether the bank provided enough information to investors about the amount of exposure its Point Bonita Capital fund had to First Brands, according to CNBC.

data.title: CNH Industrial cut to Sell equivalent at JPM after Deere's dismal ag equipment outlook
data.description:
CNH Industrial (CNH) +0.5% in Friday's trading even as J.P. Morgan downgraded shares the agricultural equipment maker to Underweight from Neutral with a $10 price target, following a negative ag industry outlook from Deere (DE), whose management introduced its FY 2026 guidance for net income of $4.0 billion-$4.75 billion vs. prior consensus of $5.3 billion, implying FY 2026 earnings of ~$16/share at the midpoint vs. prior consensus of $19.98/share.

JPM analyst Tami Zakaria said she expects another down year in North America large ag in 2026, and her estimates remain below consensus for both FY 2025 and FY 2026, so with Deere (DE) trading at 4x-5x above smaller peers, she views the risk/reward for CNH Industrial (CNH) as evenly balanced at current valuation.

The analyst believes AGCO (AGCO) is the only ag firm in its coverage area that could see flattish earnings next year given more than half of its revenues come from Europe and the Mediterranean coupled with possible buybacks, while both Deere (DE) and CNH (CNH) should see declines.

data.title: CAE Inc. gains amid takeover speculation
data.description:
• CAE Inc. (CAE) rose 1.3% amid takeover speculation for the Canadian aerospace and defense company.
  • There's speculation that CAE has attracted takeover interest, according to a Betaville "uncooked" alert that was circulating on Friday. There's talk that CAE has received interest from a U.S. private equity firm that has a history of investments in the defense and aerospace industries. 
  • CAE (CAE) has a market cap of $8.5 billion.

data.title: Markets remain ‘pretty strong’ heading into the next months – MS Wealth Management’s Lacamp
data.description:
As the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) faces the end of a seven-month win streak and the Dow (DJI) and S&P 500 (SP500) struggle to maintain six-month winning streaks, market conditions remain “pretty strong” heading into the year-end and likely through February, according to Morgan Stanley Wealth Management senior vice president Jim Lacamp.

During a CNBC interview, he characterized the current market as a “rodeo bull,” noting its volatility while maintaining an overall bullish outlook.

“It’s been a very volatile market and very news driven market, particularly in the AI space,” Lacamp said. “But the market overall, the set of conditions is really pretty strong as we look towards the end of the year and probably through February where the seasonals can change.”

He cited returning buyers, including significant volume from the hedge fund community last Friday and Monday, as positive indicators.

Interest rate expectations are also bolstering market sentiment, with Fed funds futures showing almost an 87% probability of a December rate cut.

“It would be very unusual for a market in a seasonally strong time of year with the Fed cutting rates, earnings on the increase, productivity and margins on the increase, to have a weak time frame,” Lacamp explained, though he noted Morgan Stanley’s official position differs from his own assessment.

The strategist advised investors to be mindful of ongoing market rotation, with value sectors like financials (XLF), energy (XLE), and healthcare (XLV) beginning to show relative strength.

“There is some rotation going on in the market,” he said, cautioning that while “the growth is still there” in technology (XLK), “these stocks aren’t cheap” and may need to consolidate. He specifically warned investors to “stay away from unprofitable names” and “meme stocks.”

Looking toward next year, Lacamp expressed cautious optimism despite historical weakness in midterm election years.

“Historically, the second year of a presidential cycle, you get quite a bit of a correction,” he acknowledged, while suggesting that tax cuts and deregulation could offset typical weakness.

He projected modest GDP growth around 2% and a market that ends higher overall, though likely with more volatility than this year’s steady gains.
